metaTitle: Developers — Tribulation | API for Mod Integration
metaDescription: Tribulation's read-only public API and level-change event for mod developers: read a player's level and tier, react to level changes, and integrate as a soft dependency.
ogDescription: Read-only public API and level-change event for integrating with Tribulation.
title: Developers
lede: Tribulation exposes a stable, read-only API and a level-change event so other mods can read a player's difficulty level and react to it — without a hard dependency.

html: Compile against Tribulation with modCompileOnly so it stays optional at runtime, then guard every call with a mod-loaded check.
Guard calls so your mod loads cleanly whether or not Tribulation is present:
Static entry points on com.rfizzle.tribulation.api.TribulationAPI. Server-side methods are authoritative; the client method reads the last value synced to that client.

rows: The player's current tier (0–5), derived from level and the tier thresholds.
The level that would drive scaling for this entity's position (nearest-player based). Returns 0 off-server.
The local player's last-synced level. Returns −1 if unknown or the mod isn't present.
The tier the entity was scaled to at spawn. Empty if never scaled.
Check if an entity was scaled by Tribulation.

html: Control the drop chance of armor and weapons equipped by Tribulation. This allows external loot-modifying mods to override the default drop rules.

items.title: Armor Drop Chance

html: Providers are "last writer wins". If a provider throws an exception or returns a non-finite value (NaN/Infinity), the API automatically falls back to the configured default chance.

body.html: The callback fires server-side whenever a player's level changes — from playtime, death relief, Shatter Shards, or an operator command.

items: The API package is com.rfizzle.tribulation.api — everything outside it is internal and may change without notice.
Server-side methods require a ServerPlayer/Entity on a running server; call them from server logic, not client rendering.
getClientLevel() is reflection-backed so the server-side API surface carries no compile-time dependency on client-only classes — it's safe to reference from common code.
Tribulation uses official Mojang mappings (e.g. ServerPlayer, Component), matching a standard 1.21.1 Fabric + Loom setup.

html: Building an integration and something's missing? Open an issue — the API surface grows based on real integration needs.
